parsed_review.summary: CP00-598 is a descriptor-only closeout pack covering RP19.P06.M05.S21-RP19.P06.M06.S08 (10 units: 2 Permission And Audit Binding tail rows + 8 Synthetic Fixture Set head rows). Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms full cross-artifact consistency among the registry binding (CLIENT_PORTAL_CORE_CP598_PACK_BINDING), manifest.json, review-baseline.json, the contract (current_pack, p06_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor, cp598_requirements, cp598_no_write_attestation, historical_pack_bindings), the validator script, the test suite, and the README: pack_id CP00-598, risk_class A, unit_count 10, range RP19.P06.M05.S21-RP19.P06.M06.S08, upstream CP00-597, next CP00-599, next_subphase RP19.P06.M06.S09, and deliverable distribution test:2/security_audit:2/implementation:6. All 7 required public exports exist (createClientPortalCoreCp598P06PermissionFixtureSliceCaseSet/Descriptor in service.js; validateClientPortalCoreCp598Coverage, validateClientPortalCoreCp598P06PermissionFixtureSliceDescriptor, and the Hermes/Claude/Closeout packet creators in validators.js) and are surfaced through index.js. Coverage and slice validators correctly enforce unit counts, phase/micro/deliverable distributions, section rows, descriptor-only guards, authority boundary, and the no-write attestation. Every row across both M05 and M06 sections (including the two security_audit deliverables Permission matrix row and Audit hint fields, plus the sensitive AI retrieval decision binding) is descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, with synthetic_only/no_real_data and no runtime dispatch, no state or audit writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no cross-tenant access, and no permission/audit/blocked-claim/fixture-payload leak. The production_ready gate is not weakened: production_ready and runtime_ready are both false and the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification marker rather than a runtime trust claim. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not a final approver (descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval:false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, Claude packet read_only:true), and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling (H19), not product runtime. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte valid JSON stdout, non-zero), prompt is 2166 bytes within the 25600-byte limit, no forbidden tools, source inspected via read tools (summary-only correctly prohibited because security/audit deliverables are present), and no active untracked implementation files were omitted. The changed-file scope correctly separates the eight active client-portal/contract/validator refs from unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room artifacts and workbook docs), which are not part of this pack. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; the pack-level production_ready (descriptor-verified) claim is supported by implementation, tests, recorded Hermes/local validation, and the no-write/no-real-data boundaries.
